task_completion.description: Verify the patient's weight is corrected and missing vitals are recorded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Bahmni Observations/Vitals section, look for Weight: 68 kg (corrected from 185), Temperature: 38.7 C, Respiratory Rate: 24, SpO2: 93%, and Blood Pressure: 100/65 mmHg.

task_completion.description: Verify the contraindicated Amoxicillin order is stopped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Medications/Treatments section, Amoxicillin 500mg should be marked as stopped, discontinued, or inactive.

task_completion.description: Verify a safe, non-penicillin, non-cephalosporin antibiotic is prescribed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Medications/Treatments section, look for a new active order for a safe antibiotic (e.g., Azithromycin, Levofloxacin, Doxycycline, Clarithromycin). There must NOT be any active orders for penicillins (amoxicillin, ampicillin) or cephalosporins (cephalexin, ceftriaxone, etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify the correct diagnoses are documented with appropriate order and certainty.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Diagnoses section, look for 'Pneumonia' marked as PRIMARY and CONFIRMED, and 'Sepsis' marked as SECONDARY and PRESUMED.

task_completion.description: Verify the patient disposition is set to Admit Patient with the correct note.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Disposition section, the status should be 'Admit Patient' and the accompanying note should read 'ICU admission for sepsis management'.

task_completion.description: Verify a follow-up appointment is scheduled for 7 days in the future.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Appointments module or patient dashboard, look for an upcoming OPD appointment scheduled approximately 7 days from the current system date.

task_completion.description: Verify a clinical encounter note of sufficient length is written.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Consultation Notes or Observation section, look for a text note documenting the corrections and care plan. It should visually appear to be at least 200 characters long (typically 3-4 full lines of text or a detailed paragraph).

integrity.description: The agent must use the Bahmni web interface to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Bahmni EMR web interface in a browser, not terminal windows, API testing tools (like Postman), or database consoles.

integrity.description: The agent must perform all actions on the correct patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient header in the screenshots must clearly show 'Amina Nyong'o' and/or the identifier 'BAH000030'.

integrity.description: The agent must actually enter the data into the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data should be visible within the proper Bahmni UI elements (forms, tables, dashboards) rather than just typed into a notepad or injected via browser developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Amina Nyong'o (BAH000030). Target Vitals: Weight 68 kg, Temp 38.7 C, RR 24/min, SpO2 93%, BP 100/65 mmHg. Medication rules: Amoxicillin MUST be stopped due to Penicillin allergy. Safe alternatives include Azithromycin, Levofloxacin, Clarithromycin, Doxycycline, etc. Penicillins and cephalosporins are strictly blacklisted. Diagnoses: Pneumonia (Primary, Confirmed) and Sepsis (Secondary, Presumed). Disposition: 'Admit Patient' with note 'ICU admission for sepsis management'. Appointment: OPD follow-up in 7 days. Note: Must be >= 200 characters.
